evidence: The paper explicitly interprets importance magnitude and ranking to identify "drivers of microclimatic variability": local macroclimatic data "exerted the most substantial influence, with its influence ranging from 50 to 80%"; TMIN at station is ranked #1 with 68.3% importance for soil temperature prediction; solar radiation, FAPAR, NDMI emerge as "key predictors" based on relative importance percentages. No alternative importance methods (permutation, SHAP, PDP/ALE, conditional) are used to corroborate these rankings.
